task_completion.description: Enable Microsoft Defender SmartScreen in Edge
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Edge Settings page (Privacy, search, and services) with the toggle for 'Microsoft Defender SmartScreen' turned on.
Screenshots show the Edge Settings page (Profiles -> Passwords) with the toggle for 'Offer to save passwords' turned off.

task_completion.description: Disable Address and Form Autofill in Edge
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Edge Settings page (Profiles -> Personal info) with the toggle for 'Save and fill basic info' or 'Save and fill custom info' turned off.

task_completion.description: Set Default Search Engine to DuckDuckGo in Edge
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Edge Settings page (Privacy, search, and services -> Address bar and search) with the 'Search engine used in the address bar' dropdown set to 'DuckDuckGo'.

task_completion.description: Set Tracking Prevention Level to Strict in Edge
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Edge Settings page (Privacy, search, and services) with the 'Strict' option selected under Tracking prevention.

task_completion.description: Create the compliance confirmation report with required details
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal creating/editing '/home/ga/Desktop/compliance_report.txt'. The file must contain a list of the 5 requirements, the specific settings changed, and their navigation locations within the Edge Settings interface.

integrity.description: Agent actually read the security policy document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening or reading the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/security_policy.txt' (e.g., using cat, less, or a text editor) before making changes.

integrity.description: Agent used the Microsoft Edge GUI to change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must open Microsoft Edge and navigate through its graphical settings menu. Modifying Edge configuration files directly via terminal (e.g., editing JSON files in ~/.config/microsoft-edge) without opening the browser GUI is a violation.

integrity.description: Agent wrote the compliance report based on actual GUI navigation
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should manually type or construct the compliance report in a text editor or via terminal commands, reflecting the actual settings locations seen in the GUI, rather than running a pre-written script to generate the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The security policy requires 5 specific Edge settings to be configured: 1) Microsoft Defender SmartScreen must be ENABLED. 2) Password Manager must be DISABLED. 3) Address and Form Autofill must be DISABLED. 4) Default search engine must be set to DuckDuckGo. 5) Tracking prevention must be set to Strict mode. The compliance report must be located at /home/ga/Desktop/compliance_report.txt and list each requirement, the specific setting changed, and its location within the Edge Settings interface.
